warmth
you can
carry
Chinese-inspired dehydrated trail meals. Just add boiling water.
coming soon ↓
our story
Every trail meal I've ever packed tasted like someone else's kitchen
Before the restaurant doors opened every morning, my family ate congee. Simple rice, ginger, whatever was on hand. It was the meal that started every day. Quiet, warm, restorative.
Years later, I kept thinking about that bowl. Nothing in the trail food aisle came close. So I made it portable. Lucky Bag is that morning bowl, made for wherever your day takes you.
minutes
Boil water, pour into the pouch, wait five minutes. Hot, satisfying, real food at camp or on the trail.
flavors
From ginger & scallion to century egg & pork, each flavor is rooted in a Chinese culinary tradition.
compromises
You shouldn't have to choose between hitting the trail and eating food that feels like yours.
the lineup
Six flavors. All congee.
Each pouch is a different Chinese flavor tradition, color coded so you always grab the right one.
